description: Local development environments — containers, devcontainers, lightweight K8s, and dev tooling

tools.name: Dev Containers
tools.description: VS Code / IDE-native containerized dev environments. Defined in .devcontainer/devcontainer.json.
tools.best_for: Consistent dev environments across team — works on any OS, any machine
gotchas: Docker Desktop required — adds RAM overhead (2-4GB baseline)
Extension compatibility — not all VS Code extensions work in containers
File sync on macOS is slow for large node_modules — use named volumes
anti_patterns: Putting the entire production stack in one devcontainer — use compose for multi-service
tools.version_note: Spec 0.250+ stable, supported in VS Code, IntelliJ, Codespaces, Gitpod

tools.name: K3s
tools.description: Lightweight Kubernetes by Rancher. Single binary, ~512MB RAM, production-grade.
tools.best_for: Local K8s testing when you need real K8s APIs but not full cluster overhead
gotchas: Default uses SQLite not etcd — fine for dev, not for HA
Traefik is default ingress — may conflict if you expect nginx
Some CRDs from cloud providers won't work — test compatibility
anti_patterns: Using K3s in production without understanding the tradeoffs vs full K8s
tools.docker: rancher/k3s:latest — or install via curl script
tools.version_note: Tracks K8s releases closely, ~1 month lag

tools.name: Tilt
tools.description: Smart rebuilds for K8s dev. Watches files, builds images, updates pods — live reload for containers.
tools.best_for: Microservice development on K8s with fast feedback loops
gotchas: Tiltfile (Starlark/Python-like) has a learning curve
Requires a running K8s cluster (K3s, minikube, or remote)
Image build caching is critical — without it, rebuilds are slow
anti_patterns: Using Tilt for single-container apps — docker-compose is simpler
tools.docker: N/A — CLI tool, works with any K8s cluster

tools.name: Skaffold
tools.description: Google's CI/CD and dev tool for K8s. Build, push, deploy in one command.
tools.best_for: GKE-native teams wanting unified dev-to-prod pipeline tooling
gotchas: Config complexity grows with multi-service setups — profiles help
File sync mode is faster than full rebuild but has limitations
Google-centric — works elsewhere but best with GKE/Cloud Build
anti_patterns: Using Skaffold just for local dev when Tilt gives better live-reload UX

tools.name: Minikube
tools.description: Official local K8s cluster. Runs in VM or container. Full K8s API compatibility.
tools.best_for: Learning K8s, testing manifests, when you need exact K8s API compatibility
gotchas: Heavier than K3s — 2GB+ RAM default
VM driver choice matters — Docker driver is fastest, HyperKit/VirtualBox are slower
Addons (dashboard, ingress, metrics) each add resource overhead
anti_patterns: Running minikube in CI — use Kind (K8s in Docker) for CI pipelines instead

tools.name: Kind (K8s in Docker)
tools.description: Kubernetes clusters using Docker containers as nodes. Designed for testing K8s itself.
tools.best_for: CI pipelines, integration testing, multi-node cluster simulation
gotchas: No persistent storage by default — data lost when cluster deleted
LoadBalancer services don't work — use NodePort or ingress
Multi-node clusters eat Docker resources fast
anti_patterns: Using Kind for day-to-day development — Tilt+K3s gives better DX
tools.docker: kindest/node:v1.x — one container per K8s node

tools.name: Mutagen
tools.description: Fast, bidirectional file sync. Solves macOS Docker bind mount performance problem.
tools.best_for: macOS developers with large codebases in Docker — eliminates the volume mount slowness
gotchas: Adds a sync daemon — one more thing to manage
Conflict resolution on bidirectional sync needs attention
Free tier is limited — Mutagen Compose is commercial
anti_patterns: Using Mutagen on Linux — bind mounts are already fast on Linux

decision_matrix.signal: Team needs consistent dev environment across OS
decision_matrix.why: IDE-native, works on any OS, .devcontainer in repo

decision_matrix.signal: Microservice dev with live reload on K8s
decision_matrix.why: File watch → rebuild → deploy in seconds

decision_matrix.signal: Learning K8s, exact API compat needed
Simple multi-service local dev, no K8s needed
decision_matrix.why: Don't introduce K8s complexity if you don't need K8s
